displayTitle: Your Initials Say It's Fit to Run

beats.narration: At Level I you ran the line. At Level II you confirm it is fit to run — and your initials on the shift record say so.

payload.title: EQUIPMENT REVIEW
payload.sub: From running the line to signing it off.

beats.narration: The review covers the whole stationary line — precleaning through developer and the booth — plus the aerosol kit and UV-A lamp for the field.

payload.heading: The hardware you are signing for

beats.narration: Lighting is a measured variable. A radiometer reads UV-A irradiance; a photometer reads white-light illuminance. Each meter vouches for only its own number.
Then physics meets physiology. The dye re-emits invisible UV-A as yellow-green near five-fifty — the peak of dark-adapted vision, so faint cracks shine.

payload.heading: Why fluorescent glows yellow-green
payload.note: EMISSION ~550 nm LANDS ON THE EYE'S PEAK

beats.narration: So review is a gate, not a tour. A cracked filter or an expired sticker stops the line. If a number fails, parts wait.

payload.sub: Any line that fails stops the process.
